name: Ka'au Crater Trail
tagline: One of Oahu's most rewarding secret hikes — past three waterfalls to a volcanic crater rim with jaw-dropping panoramic views.
parking: Street parking on Waiomao Rd; respect residents
bestTime: Early morning on dry days; extremely dangerous when wet
talkStory: Ka'au is one of several small craters formed during the Ko'olau volcano's last eruptions roughly 30,000 years ago — the same rejuvenation-stage eruptions that created Diamond Head and Koko Crater. Ancient Hawaiians cultivated taro in the lower Palolo Valley, and you can still spot remnants of old lo'i terrace walls near the stream. The three waterfalls are increasingly dramatic, with the final one requiring rope-assisted climbing up a near-vertical face. Multiple rescues happen here yearly — bring gloves, allow 5-6 hours, and never attempt when wet.
